corrective_fix_applied.summary: Added SECURITY DEFINER helper public.facilitation_case_visible(_user uuid, _case uuid) with REVOKE FROM PUBLIC + GRANT EXECUTE to authenticated/service_role; replaced fevd_select / fevd_insert storage policies to call the helper instead of an inline chained EXISTS … OR is_admin … OR has_role(...) expression. Same access boundary, simpler planner shape.
corrective_fix_applied.verification: Helper returns true for Org A user + their case; new fevd_* policies registered on storage.objects (polcmd 'r' and 'a').

checks_passed: orgA.created_event_present (action=facilitation_case.created, from_status=null, to_status='new', actor_user_id=Org A user)
negative_control.no_side_effect_writes (pois=0, wads=0, matches=0, token_ledger=0, token_purchases=0, notification_dispatches=0, email_send_log=0, poi_engagements=0, audit_logs.actor_in_test_users=0)

remaining_failure.body: {"statusCode":"503","error":"DatabaseInvalidObjectDefinition","message":"The database schema is invalid or incompatible."}
remaining_failure.root_cause_revised: NOT the fevd_* policies. The helper fix is correctly in place and the new fevd_* policies compile cleanly. The actual root cause is a pre-existing infinite-recursion defect between two RLS policies that storage.objects must evaluate when ANY upload occurs:
recursion_chain: storage.objects policy 'View match documents based on visibility' (SELECT) → subquery on public.match_documents
public.match_documents policy 'Document visibility based on ownership and sharing' (SELECT) → subquery on public.document_access
public.document_access policy 'Users can view access grants for their documents' (SELECT) → subquery on public.match_documents
remaining_failure.log_evidence: postgres_logs at 2026-06-13T18:01:26Z (mid-run): 'infinite recursion detected in policy for relation "document_access"'
remaining_failure.scope_assessment: This defect is OUTSIDE the facilitation feature surface. It pre-dates Phase 1 and affects any authenticated INSERT into storage.objects whose policy set includes the match-documents SELECT policy. Confirmed by the fact that the same orgA.storage_upload failure (with identical 503 DatabaseInvalidObjectDefinition body) was present BEFORE the helper migration — the helper fix did not regress anything; it correctly addressed the suspected planner shape, but the residual is a separate, pre-existing RLS recursion bug on match_documents ↔ document_access.
remaining_failure.smallest_safe_fix_proposal: Break the match_documents ↔ document_access cycle by extracting visibility into a single SECURITY DEFINER helper, e.g. public.match_document_visible(_user uuid, _document_id uuid) RETURNS boolean, and rewriting both 'Document visibility based on ownership and sharing' (on match_documents) and 'Users can view access grants for their documents' (on document_access) to call the helper. This is a corrective fix to a pre-existing defect and changes no behaviour beyond removing the recursion.
remaining_failure.authorization_required: User explicitly authorised ONLY the facilitation-evidence storage helper fix. This second fix touches match_documents/document_access policies, which are outside the facilitation scope grant. Awaiting explicit approval before applying.
